vulnerabilities.fix: Implement input validation and output encoding to prevent cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.
Set up HTTP security headers like Content Security Policy (CSP), Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), and X-Content-Type-Options to enhance website security.
Implement X-Frame-Options header with 'DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN' directive to prevent clickjacking attacks.

vulnerabilities.flaw: Exposed sensitive information in error messages
vulnerabilities.fix: Avoid displaying detailed error messages that expose sensitive information to users. Instead, provide generic error messages and log detailed errors on the server side for diagnostic purposes.
Regularly update and patch software components like web servers, frameworks, and libraries to mitigate known security vulnerabilities.
Implement strict transport security (HSTS) header and Content Security Policy (CSP) header to protect against various attacks such as XSS and clickjacking.
Implement input validation and output encoding to prevent malicious scripts from being executed on the site.
Enforce strong password requirements such as minimum length, complexity, and regular password updates to enhance user account security.
Implement two-factor authentication to add an extra layer of security to user accounts and prevent unauthorized access.
Implement frame busting code to prevent the site from being embedded in iframes on malicious sites, protecting against clickjacking attacks.

flaws.description: The website may be using outdated software which could contain known vulnerabilities.
flaws.recommendation: Regularly update and patch all software components including web servers, CMS, plugins, and other third-party libraries.
flaws.fix-law: Ensure all software components are updated to the latest secure versions.

flaws.description: The website may be vulnerable to cross-site scripting attacks.
flaws.recommendation: Implement proper input validation and output encoding to prevent XSS attacks.
flaws.fix-law: Implement strict input validation and output encoding to mitigate XSS vulnerabilities.

flaws.description: The website may not be using secure communication protocols such as HTTPS.
flaws.recommendation: Enforce the use of HTTPS for all web traffic to encrypt data in transit.
flaws.fix-law: Configure the web server to use HTTPS and ensure all traffic is encrypted.

flaws.description: The website may be exposing sensitive data such as user information or payment details.
flaws.recommendation: Implement strong data encryption for sensitive information and limit access to such data.
flaws.fix-law: Ensure sensitive data is encrypted and implement access controls to protect against data exposure.

flaws.description: The website may be missing important security headers such as Content Security Policy (CSP) and X-Content-Type-Options.
flaws.recommendation: Add security headers to enhance website security and protect against various types of attacks.
flaws.fix-law: Implement security headers such as Content Security Policy (CSP) and X-Content-Type-Options to improve website security.

vulnerabilities.fix: Implement security headers such as Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, and Strict-Transport-Security to protect against various attacks like XSS, clickjacking, and man-in-the-middle attacks.
Regularly update all software components including CMS, plugins, and server software to the latest versions to patch known vulnerabilities and reduce the risk of exploitation.
Implement input validation mechanisms to sanitize user inputs and encode user-generated content before rendering it in the browser to prevent XSS attacks.
Set the 'Secure' flag on cookies to ensure they are only sent over HTTPS connections, and use the 'HttpOnly' flag to prevent client-side scripts from accessing them.
Disable directory listing in web server configuration to prevent attackers from enumerating the directory structure and accessing sensitive information.
